campaigns.name: Planning Analytics — New Whitespace Florida
campaigns.tagline: Move from reactive planning to proactive, insight-driven decision-making.

emails.subject: Your forecast cycle — quick question
emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

Most finance teams I speak with are still manually rebuilding forecasts every time conditions shift.

IBM Planning Analytics runs on an in-memory database — real-time what-if scenarios and instant model updates as inputs change. No waiting, no data science team required.

Worth 20 minutes to see how it applies to [Company]?

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida

emails.subject: One model. Every source. Plain language.
emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

Following up from last week.

Planning Analytics integrates data from spreadsheets, Oracle, SAP, and other EPM systems into one model — then lets your team ask questions in plain language and get instant answers, reports, and narratives.

No analyst bottleneck. Finance, ops, and business teams all working from the same data.

Open to a quick look?

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida

emails.subject: AI that plans — without losing control
emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

One more thought on Planning Analytics.

AI agents now monitor your plan continuously and automate repetitive workflows — while granular security controls ensure only the right people see the right data.

Move faster without sacrificing governance. Closed-loop planning built for enterprise environments.

15 minutes to see it?

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida

emails.subject: Last note — IBM Planning Analytics
emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

Last touch on Planning Analytics.

Built for large, complex enterprises: real-time in-memory speed, integrates Oracle, SAP, and spreadsheets, AI-augmented automation, and granular data security — all in one platform.

If modernizing your planning environment is on [Company]'s radar this year, I'd love to connect.

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida

campaigns.name: Planning Analytics — Expansion (Existing IBM)

emails.subject: What's new in Planning Analytics
emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

Since [Company] is already on Planning Analytics, wanted to flag what's new.

The AI layer now handles forecasting, narrative generation, and workflow automation — all running on the same in-memory engine and within the governance and security controls your team already has in place.

Worth a quick sync to walk through what's available?

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida

emails.subject: Natural language planning — PA update
emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

Following up on Planning Analytics.

New generative AI capability lets your team query planning data in plain language — across all integrated sources including spreadsheets, Oracle, and SAP. Instant answers and auto-generated reports without adding headcount.

Broader participation across finance, ops, and business teams.

Open to 20 minutes?

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida

persona_emails.subject: Finance planning — quick question
persona_emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

Most CFOs I speak with are dealing with the same challenge — disconnected planning and forecast cycles that can't keep up with how fast conditions change.

IBM Planning Analytics unifies financial planning on one real-time platform, integrating Oracle, SAP, and spreadsheets so your FP&A team moves faster with full confidence in the numbers.

Worth 20 minutes for [Company]?

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida

persona_emails.pain_point: Lack of confidence in data, error-prone processes
persona_emails.subject: Consolidation confidence — quick note
persona_emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

Controllers I speak with often cite the same issue: error-prone consolidation and a lack of confidence in the numbers at close.

IBM Planning Analytics automates monthly, quarterly, and annual consolidation with granular data controls — reducing manual errors and giving your team a single source of truth.

Worth 20 minutes?

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida

persona_emails.persona: COO / VP Operations / Supply Chain VP
persona_emails.subject: Ops planning data — quick thought
persona_emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

Operations leaders I speak with consistently flag the same challenge: data discrepancies across departments making supply chain and capacity planning unreliable.

IBM Planning Analytics integrates every data source into one real-time model — supply chain, inventory, capacity, and asset planning all in sync.

Worth 20 minutes for [Company]?

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida

persona_emails.pain_point: Lack of agility in workforce planning
persona_emails.subject: Workforce planning — quick question
persona_emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

HR leaders I speak with struggle with the same thing: headcount and compensation planning that can't keep pace with how fast the business moves.

IBM Planning Analytics brings real agility to workforce planning — real-time scenario modeling for headcount, salary, and staffing decisions at scale.

Worth 20 minutes?

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida

persona_emails.subject: Sales planning on intuition?
persona_emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

Sales leaders I speak with tell me the same thing — intuition still drives 2 out of 3 planning decisions around territory, quota, and capacity.

IBM Planning Analytics replaces gut feel with real-time data modeling, so your team forecasts, plans territories, and sets quotas with confidence.

Worth 20 minutes for [Company]?

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida

persona_emails.subject: Marketing planning in siloes?
persona_emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

Marketing leaders I speak with are dealing with disconnected planning — campaign and promotion data living in tools that don't talk to each other or the rest of the business.

IBM Planning Analytics breaks down analytical siloes, connecting marketing planning to finance, sales, and ops in real time.

Worth 20 minutes?

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida

persona_emails.pain_point: Disconnected IT portfolio and budget planning

persona_emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

CIOs I speak with flag the same challenge: IT portfolio and budget planning stuck in disconnected systems with no unified view.

IBM Planning Analytics unifies IT project planning, budgeting, and infrastructure planning on one platform — with the enterprise-grade security and scalability your team requires.
